_instructions: This is a locale-agnostic starter template for an eviction/tenancy defense navigator. Fork this file, replace every value marked with [REPLACE] with your jurisdiction's actual information, and remove all '_' prefixed keys before publishing. See README.md for a full authoring guide.
jurisdiction: [REPLACE: ISO 3166 code, e.g. US-NY-NYC, GB-ENG-LDN, KE-NBO, ZA-WC]
language: [REPLACE: BCP 47 language tag, e.g. en, es, fr, sw, pt-BR]

civicData._note: All values here become available as {{placeholders}} in node text and item text. Add as many fields as your jurisdiction needs.
civicData.jurisdiction_name: [REPLACE: Human-readable name, e.g. 'Nairobi, Kenya' or 'England and Wales']
civicData.primary_statute: [REPLACE: The main tenancy/housing statute, e.g. 'Landlord and Tenant Act 1985 (UK)' or 'Residential Tenancies Act 2004 (Ireland)']
civicData.notice_days_nonpayment: [REPLACE: Days tenant has to pay rent before landlord can file, e.g. '7']
civicData.notice_days_violation: [REPLACE: Days tenant has to fix a lease violation, e.g. '14']
civicData.hearing_window_days: [REPLACE: Typical days between filing and hearing, e.g. '14-21']
civicData.appeal_days: [REPLACE: Days after judgment to file an appeal, e.g. '10']
civicData.court_name: [REPLACE: Name of the court that handles residential evictions]
civicData.court_address: [REPLACE: Full street address of the eviction court]
civicData.legal_aid_name: [REPLACE: Primary free legal aid organization name]
civicData.legal_aid_eligibility: [REPLACE: Who qualifies, e.g. 'Households below 125% of federal poverty level']
civicData.emergency_housing_phone: [REPLACE: Emergency housing or homeless prevention hotline]
civicData.rental_assistance_url: [REPLACE: URL to apply for emergency rental assistance, if available]
civicData.selfhelp_url: [REPLACE: Court or government self-help resource URL]

start._note: Entry point. Tenant describes their situation. Adjust option labels to match local terminology.
start.text: [REPLACE: Opening question in plain language, e.g. 'What have you received from your landlord or the court?']

options.label: [REPLACE: e.g. 'A written notice from my landlord']
[REPLACE: e.g. 'Court papers scheduling a hearing']
[REPLACE: e.g. 'Nothing yet — I am worried about falling behind on rent']

notice_type._note: Branch on type of notice received. Add or remove options to match notice types in your jurisdiction.

options.label: [REPLACE: e.g. 'Pay rent within {{notice_days_nonpayment}} days or vacate']
[REPLACE: e.g. 'Fix a lease violation or vacate']
[REPLACE: e.g. 'Vacate — no reason given']
[REPLACE: e.g. 'I am not sure what it says']

notice_pay._note: Nonpayment notice. Surface the deadline and ask if tenant can pay.
notice_pay.text: [REPLACE: e.g. 'You have {{notice_days_nonpayment}} days to pay all rent owed under {{primary_statute}}. Can you pay in full?']

options.label: [REPLACE: e.g. 'Yes — I can pay in full']
[REPLACE: e.g. 'No — I cannot pay in full']
[REPLACE: e.g. 'I may qualify for housing assistance']

notice_violation._note: Lease violation notice. Ask if tenant can cure it.
notice_violation.text: [REPLACE: e.g. 'Can you fix the lease violation within {{notice_days_violation}} days?']

options.label: [REPLACE: e.g. 'Yes — I can fix it']
[REPLACE: e.g. 'No — or I dispute the violation']

notice_no_cause._note: No-cause or no-fault notice. Surface any just-cause protections that exist in this jurisdiction.

items.text: [REPLACE: Explain what a no-cause notice means in this jurisdiction and whether just-cause protections apply.]
[REPLACE: If retaliation protections exist, mention them here — e.g. eviction following a complaint to authorities may be unlawful.]
[REPLACE: Direct to legal aid, e.g. 'Call {{legal_aid_name}} for a free case evaluation: {{legal_aid_phone}}']

defenses_check._note: Habitability / repair defense. Adjust statute reference and description for local law.
defenses_check.text: [REPLACE: e.g. 'Has your landlord failed to make necessary repairs after you notified them in writing? Tenants may have a habitability defense under {{primary_statute}}.']

options.label: [REPLACE: e.g. 'Yes — I reported defects and they were not fixed']
[REPLACE: e.g. 'No']

retaliation_check._note: Retaliation defense. Remove this node if retaliation protections do not exist in this jurisdiction.
retaliation_check.text: [REPLACE: e.g. 'Did you recently complain to a government agency or assert your legal rights — and did this eviction follow shortly after?']

options.label: [REPLACE: e.g. 'Yes — eviction followed my complaint']
[REPLACE: e.g. 'No']

rental_assistance_check._note: Only include if rental assistance programs exist in this jurisdiction. Otherwise route directly to checklist_main.
rental_assistance_check.text: [REPLACE: e.g. 'Have you applied for emergency housing assistance?']

options.label: [REPLACE: e.g. 'Yes — application is pending']
[REPLACE: e.g. 'No — I have not applied yet']

court_received._note: Tenant has already been served court papers. Urgency node — surface hearing date and key deadlines immediately.
court_received.text: [REPLACE: e.g. 'Court papers mean your landlord has filed. Hearings are typically scheduled within {{hearing_window_days}} days. Do you know your hearing date?']

options.label: [REPLACE: e.g. 'Yes — I know my hearing date']
[REPLACE: e.g. 'No — it is not clear on the papers']

find_hearing_date._note: Tenant does not know their hearing date. Direct to court immediately.

items.text: [REPLACE: e.g. 'Call {{court_name}} immediately to confirm your hearing date and time: {{court_phone}}']
[REPLACE: e.g. 'Court address: {{court_address}}']
[REPLACE: e.g. 'Missing your hearing results in automatic judgment against you — even if you have valid defenses.']
[REPLACE: e.g. 'Call {{legal_aid_name}} for same-day help: {{legal_aid_phone}}']

preemptive_advice._note: Tenant has not yet received a notice but is worried. Preventive guidance.

items.text: [REPLACE: e.g. 'Contact your landlord in writing immediately. A written payment plan offer can sometimes prevent a court filing.']
[REPLACE: e.g. 'Apply for emergency housing assistance TODAY.']
[REPLACE: e.g. 'Call {{legal_aid_name}} for free advice: {{legal_aid_phone}}']
[REPLACE: e.g. 'Save all written communications with your landlord.']

get_help_now._note: Tenant cannot identify their notice type. Route to legal aid immediately.

items.text: [REPLACE: e.g. 'Call {{legal_aid_name}} immediately — free legal help: {{legal_aid_phone}}']
[REPLACE: e.g. 'Do not ignore any notice. Eviction timelines move quickly in {{jurisdiction_name}}.']
[REPLACE: e.g. 'Self-help resources: {{selfhelp_url}}']

checklist_pay_now._note: Tenant can pay. Action checklist to stop eviction by paying.
checklist_pay_now.phase: [REPLACE: e.g. 'Action: Pay Rent to Stop Eviction']

items.text: [REPLACE: e.g. 'Pay the full rent owed within {{notice_days_nonpayment}} days of the notice date under {{primary_statute}}.']
[REPLACE: e.g. 'Pay by traceable method — money order, bank transfer, or receipt-generating method. Keep proof.']
[REPLACE: e.g. 'Get written confirmation from your landlord that the debt is satisfied.']
[REPLACE: e.g. 'If your landlord refuses payment or files in court anyway, call {{legal_aid_name}}: {{legal_aid_phone}}']

checklist_cure._note: Tenant can fix the lease violation. Action checklist.

items.text: [REPLACE: e.g. 'Fix the violation within {{notice_days_violation}} days and document it with photos, receipts, and dated records.']
[REPLACE: e.g. 'Notify your landlord in writing that the violation has been cured — keep a paper trail.']
[REPLACE: e.g. 'If your landlord files in court after you cured, that is a defense.']

checklist_apply_assistance._note: Tenant has not applied for rental assistance. Direct them to apply.

items.text: [REPLACE: e.g. 'Apply for emergency housing assistance immediately — do not wait.']
[REPLACE: e.g. 'Notify your landlord in writing that your application is pending.']
[REPLACE: e.g. 'Call {{legal_aid_name}} for free legal help: {{legal_aid_phone}}']

checklist_main._note: Full action checklist for tenants facing a hearing. This is the most important terminal node. Customize urgency and text for your jurisdiction's specific traps and deadlines.

items.text: [REPLACE: Most urgent action — e.g. 'URGENT: Appear at your {{court_name}} hearing. Missing it means automatic judgment against you.']
[REPLACE: e.g. 'Court: {{court_address}}']
[REPLACE: e.g. 'Bring to court: your lease, rent receipts, written repair requests, and all landlord communications.']
[REPLACE: Add any jurisdiction-specific procedural trap here — e.g. court registry deposits, answer filing deadlines, mandatory mediation.]
[REPLACE: e.g. 'If the judge rules against you, you have {{appeal_days}} days to file an appeal.']
[REPLACE: e.g. 'Call {{legal_aid_name}} for free representation: {{legal_aid_phone}}']
[REPLACE: e.g. 'Self-help resources: {{selfhelp_url}}']
